230548.explanation: No, the municipality must focus on its core tasks. Tax increases only out of necessity.

230549.explanation: Entrepreneurs are important for Staphorst. Therefore, the SGP wants enough space in business parks, so companies can grow and jobs remain. The municipality ensures timely expansion and good support for entrepreneurs.

230550.explanation: No, Sunday rest could be endangered. Residents also have a right to peace and privacy in the evening and at night; rules are necessary for this.

230551.explanation: Yes, Sunday rest is of great importance. Church attendance and a collective moment of rest with family and fellow humans are central. The other days of the week provide sufficient room for shopping.

230552.explanation: Nature already has a prominent place in the municipality with various existing nature areas. Agricultural land is scarce and important for food production. It must be used sparingly.

230553.explanation: For newcomers without residence status, extra language education will raise false expectations about residence. There is already language education for newcomers with residence permits; the emphasis must be on their own effort.

230554.explanation: An asylum seekers center does not fit in Staphorst. We want to minimize shelter for newcomers without status. The national government should not shift this responsibility to municipalities.

230555.explanation: Shrubs and trees are essential landscape elements. The municipality of Staphorst ensures through stimulating greening policies their preservation and increase, making mandatory replanting upon felling unnecessary.

230557.explanation: The swimming pool is an important basic facility that we want to make future-proof.

230558.explanation: The municipality ensures the maintenance of swimming facilities. Swimming skills are necessary for everyone; therefore, swimming lessons should be offered through schools.

230560.explanation: The regional language is one of the characteristics of our beautiful municipality. It is part of the intangible heritage that we must cherish. Youth should therefore be made familiar with it.

230562.explanation: The SGP believes that our own residents should be given priority. We mainly build for Staphorst residents and want social rental homes to be first available to people who live, work, or are rooted here. This keeps our villages livable and accessible for our own community.

230563.explanation: The SGP does not want a fixed 30% norm. We build what is really needed locally and constantly monitor the demand for social rental housing. This keeps the pace of construction and ensures that homes match the needs of our own residents.

230565.explanation: The SGP believes that those who buy a home should live in it themselves. This prevents buy-ups by investors and keeps housing affordable for our own residents. This helps keep our villages livable and makes housing available for Staphorst residents.

230566.explanation: The SGP wants to abolish both the aesthetic regulations and the estate layout plans. These cause unnecessary delays and paperwork. By removing them, building and renovating will be faster, clearer, and simpler for residents and entrepreneurs.

230567.explanation: The SGP does not want to use holiday homes as permanent residences. We prefer to build enough suitable homes for the elderly within the villages. Holiday parks are intended for recreation and are not suitable as a structural housing solution.

230568.explanation: No, coffeeshops sell drugs that are harmful to everyone's health. No normalization by allowing sales. Especially for youth, the barriers must not be lowered. The use of these substances should be countered from a health perspective and to prevent crime.

230570.explanation: The municipality will not automatically invest more in youth work. It is more important that existing offerings are properly appreciated and supported, and where necessary, adjusted appropriately.

230571.explanation: The municipality has a facilitating role when it comes to charging points. Operation belongs to commercial parties, who will always follow demand with their offerings.

230572.explanation: Yes, that is how we distinguish ourselves in the region. It is a service towards the customers of our shops and other visitors. This is also positive for local business activity.

230573.explanation: The SGP believes that public transport in Staphorst must improve. We focus on an express bus and a train stop, but are also open to solutions that truly help residents. Tailored transport may fit if it improves accessibility between centers.

230575.explanation: All centers within the municipality should have access to a socio-cultural community center, but this does not necessarily have to be a separate building in every village.

230576.explanation: The municipality must continue to provide free relationship therapy, so relationships are strengthened and families remain stable. Moreover, the family forms the basis for a strong society.

230577.explanation: Children and families who temporarily need care must not wait unnecessarily. The municipality therefore encourages foster parents and support families to quickly provide appropriate care.

230578.explanation: The municipality must make resources available for education about the risks of sharing sexually explicit messages. So that young people are equipped to responsibly handle media and learn to avoid risks.

230579.explanation: Emergency services increasingly have to respond to reports about confused individuals. The municipality must invest in shared living spaces for confused persons, so they receive appropriate care and have a safe environment.

230581.explanation: The SGP wants to invest in good elderly care, including in new living arrangements where elderly people can be cared for together. As long as it matches local needs and contributes to appropriate, affordable care for our residents.

230582.explanation: The municipality does not operate riding schools. Upon a request to relocate the riding school, the municipality will consider the spatial aspects.

230583.explanation: The municipality has two event sites and that is sufficient.

230584.explanation: Yes, and at the same time also for existing entrepreneurs.

230585.explanation: By keeping the living environment clean, schools can make a valuable contribution to society. This element of stewardship also has educational and upbringing value.

230586.explanation: The municipality does not need to organize separate cycling lessons for refugees; basic skills can be taught through existing opportunities.

230587.explanation: Staphorst has a Youth Council. Political awareness is taught through the Children's Council. There are many channels to get in contact with the municipal government. A child mayor adds nothing to this.

230588.explanation: The municipality of Staphorst participates in Politiekids, so that children learn about the work of the police, become involved in their environment, and early undermining is countered.

230589.explanation: No, the municipal yard must remain at its current location. Only the storage will move to make space for cemetery expansion. The fire station must also keep its place here, centrally located in the village with many thatched roofs.

230590.explanation: Yes, the fire department building is of good quality and is located centrally in our village. Considering fire fighting for thatched roofs, fast response times are very important. And this is ensured at this location.

230592.explanation: Fishing is an important way for various residents to enjoy outdoor recreation. It also provides a healthy stimulus for youth to connect with nature. The municipality can play an important role by creating and maintaining fishing spots.

230593.explanation: The municipality supports companies that employ people with a disability, so labor participation is increased and everyone can participate to their ability.

230594.explanation: The SGP does not want family homes with only one parking space. In Staphorst almost every family has multiple cars. Too little parking space causes street nuisance and unsafe situations. We therefore build with sufficient parking, fitting how our residents actually live.

230595.explanation: In many cases, finances are not the decisive factor in the choice between conventional and organic farming. The market, and thus society, determine whether conversion is desired.

230596.explanation: This initiative counters the throwaway culture, fits Christian stewardship, and provides a meaningful pastime for people who are very handy and like to do something for society.

230597.explanation: The SGP sees that the number of single-person households is growing and believes this should be taken into account. We build what is locally needed. If demand for one-person homes increases, we adjust our plans accordingly. This way homes remain affordable and suitable for our own residents.

230600.explanation: The SGP wants extra speed controls to improve behavior. We also choose measures that contribute more to traffic safety, such as clear road design, logical speeds, and safe crossings.

230601.explanation: No, initiatives can only come from the relevant organizations if there is a need.

230602.explanation: Yes, it is a good thing that everyone works as much as possible in a suitable way. It is great that entrepreneurs care about this and want to maintain it together with the municipality.

230603.explanation: No, parking options in the center are scarce and free parking at the shops is a daily necessity. The demand for terraces in summer is small and must first emerge from the grassroots.

230604.explanation: No, professional management with administration is expensive. Moreover, independent associations must also want that; more cooperation means less independence.

230605.explanation: The choice regarding vaccination is the responsibility of the parents. Upbringing and care for children primarily lie with the family.

230606.explanation: The SGP does not want fines for vacancy. We do consider long-term vacancy undesirable but prefer to solve it with conversations, customization, and good agreements. Sometimes vacancy is temporarily necessary for renovation or sale. Fines do not help then and only cause extra rules and hassle.

230608.explanation: No, these administrative costs are specifically charged for event permits. The (commercial) applicant must pay this as fees. It is not fair to pass these costs onto the entire community.

230548.explanation: We want to limit the increase in property tax primarily to inflation correction, and we are only willing to raise it if this benefits the funding of basic provisions, such as school housing and infrastructure.

230549.explanation: The Christian Union wants room for local businesses, aiming to promote local employment. Careful landscape integration is important. There is no room for large distribution centers, data centers, or production companies that mainly employ migrant workers.

230550.explanation: It is the municipality’s task to supervise the night closing of cafes and restaurants. For the Christian Union, Sunday rest is an important value!

230551.explanation: The Christian Union treasures the collective day of rest that is still largely intact in Staphorst. Sunday as a special day with space for church visits, meeting, and relaxation. We believe the day of rest is good for everyone!

230552.explanation: In the coming years, a lot of space will be needed for new residential areas and business parks. This will come at the expense of valuable agricultural land. It is irresponsible, if only from the perspective of food security, to convert even more agricultural land into nature.

230553.explanation: In Staphorst, a lot is already being done regarding language lessons for newcomers, such as recognized refugees. We appreciate the efforts of volunteers who provide language lessons. The ChristenUnie believes it is important that they learn the language quickly so they can integrate well and participate in the labor market.

230554.explanation: For us, an asylum seekers' center (AZC) is not an end in itself, but we do feel a Christian responsibility. The reception of refugees can take place according to established agreements: we fulfill our legal duty, manage the locations ourselves, and pay for this with national government funds.

230555.explanation: Trees are important elements in our living environment and landscape. Sometimes trees have to be removed, for example for the construction of a road or a residential area. The ChristenUnie believes it is important that new trees are planted to replace felled ones so that a sufficient number of trees remain.

230557.explanation: For the ChristenUnie, the swimming pool is a basic facility. The installations are outdated and the pool consumes too much energy. We believe it is important to invest in a future-proof and sustainable swimming pool. And when this is the best option, building a new swimming pool is also open for discussion.

230558.explanation: We believe that teaching children to swim is a responsibility of parents. However, we are concerned about maintaining swimming skills. As far as we are concerned, this should be addressed in grades 7 or 8, provided there is also support from the education sector.

230560.explanation: The regional language of Staphorst, a form of Low Saxon, is an important part of our culture and history. The ChristenUnie believes it is important that attention is given to the Staphorst dialect in schools to engage new generations.

230562.explanation: The homes we build must be aligned with the needs of our own residents: that is our primary focus. This should also be the principle when building social rental housing.

230563.explanation: We build according to the needs of our residents. If it appears that fewer social rental homes and more owner-occupied homes are needed to house our own population, this can be a reason to deviate from the norm of 30% social rental housing.

230565.explanation: Buying your own home should be accessible to as many residents of the municipality of Staphorst as possible. Therefore, the ChristenUnie wants to prevent real estate investors and traders from buying homes for their own financial gain. A mandatory owner-occupancy requirement is therefore a good idea!

230566.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes the municipality must maintain control over how De Streek and the town centers look. Aesthetic regulations and estate layout plans must remain part of new initiatives in these areas. This way we jointly ensure a beautiful and attractive living environment.

230567.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation. In Staphorst, especially in the village of IJhorst, recreation is very important for the local economy. The ChristenUnie wants to strengthen the recreational function of IJhorst, and vital holiday parks are important for this.

230568.explanation: There is no room for a coffeeshop in Staphorst. The ChristenUnie wants to absolutely oppose the use of drugs, including soft drugs!

230570.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes it is important that our youth can grow up healthy and safely. We want to help and support young people facing challenges as early as possible. However, it is important that youth work aligns with the needs of society and organizations.

230571.explanation: A charging station will only be installed when there is a need within a neighborhood or near public amenities. Furthermore, we believe that placing an electric charging station is not a municipal task. The costs of an electric charging station are for the operator.

230572.explanation: Paid parking does not fit in a village like Staphorst. Free parking remains the norm and ensures that the center of Staphorst remains attractive for shopping.

230573.explanation: Accessibility of our villages is important to us. Therefore, we encourage initiatives for 'tailored transport' from the community, without the municipality having to invest. A good example is Automaatje, where volunteers transport residents for a small fee.

230575.explanation: Meeting each other is an important element in our villages. Encountering others creates connection and solidarity and prevents loneliness. Therefore, it is important that the municipality, together with society, provides places to meet. Having a 'living room' in every village is therefore a good idea.

230576.explanation: Sustainable relationships are the foundation for strong families. The ChristenUnie wants to commit to healthy and sustainable relationships and to preventing (contentious) divorces, among other things through the deployment of a Relationship and Divorce Team, consisting of involved and experienced professionals.

230577.explanation: Foster care is extremely valuable in offering warmth and stability when a child cannot be raised by their own parents. We appreciate the efforts of these foster families. Therefore, we find it important that there are enough foster families available in our municipality.

230578.explanation: Sharing intimate or boundary-crossing messages and images via digital means (such as sexting) can cause deep and lasting harm, not only to individuals but to our entire society.

230579.explanation: Confused persons often cause nuisance in the neighborhood. This requires a lot of attention from the police and municipality. It is not the municipality's task to invest in housing itself, but it is to make agreements with other organizations together so that these persons receive appropriate care and housing.

230581.explanation: Living places where elderly people live together and now or in the near future need care are valuable to us. Good examples are the Seringenhof in Rouveen and the Meestershof in Staphorst. Initiatives like these can count on the support of the ChristenUnie.

230582.explanation: The ChristenUnie favors bringing sports facilities together in central locations. Of course, involved clubs must also want this themselves. An additional advantage is that locations that become available, such as the riding school and the ice rink, may then be used as housing locations.

230583.explanation: An event site is an important facility for every village. Now that space has been found in both Staphorst and Rouveen, it is a good idea to also look for IJhorst to see what the needs are and to explore possibilities for an event site.

230584.explanation: Starters with questions should be able to contact the municipality. The municipality has an account manager for entrepreneurs who is available to answer questions from new and existing entrepreneurs. Because this works well, setting up a specific starter's desk creates no added value for us.

230585.explanation: Litter is a major nuisance for many residents. Through a subsidy scheme, we encourage schools and associations to clean up litter and thus keep Staphorst clean together.

230586.explanation: The municipality takes care of good integration of recognized refugees. Bicycle lessons are not part of this. We appreciate the efforts of volunteers who provide bicycle lessons!

230587.explanation: Children experience their living environment differently than adults. They see problems and opportunities that adults sometimes overlook, such as playgrounds, traffic safety, or bullying. A child mayor helps to put these insights on the agenda.

230588.explanation: The Politiekids project is important because it introduces children in a very accessible way to safety, responsibility, and cooperation in their own neighborhood. It contributes to the resilience of children, increases their self-confidence, and improves the neighborhood.

230589.explanation: We value the location of the current cemetery in the center of the village. Therefore, we believe that the space of the municipal yard should be used for the necessary expansion of the adjacent cemetery and not for building homes.

230590.explanation: The current location of the fire station is currently an ideal spot: central in the village and quickly accessible for all volunteer firefighters. A possible relocation of a station is only open for discussion if safety and good accessibility remain guaranteed.

230592.explanation: Fishing is a healthy way to relax in nature and, for youth, a good alternative to gaming on computers. Away from the screen and to the water’s edge! Good fishing spots are needed for this! As far as we are concerned, a fishing jetty at the Conrad opposite the Moluccan monument is a good idea.

230593.explanation: Every person is valuable and wants to be valuable! We encourage companies to employ people with disabilities.

230594.explanation: The car remains an important means of transport in our municipality. Many households have more than one (company) car. The ChristenUnie wants to maintain the current standard of two parking spaces per family home to prevent parking nuisance in residential neighborhoods.

230595.explanation: The operations of agricultural businesses are not a matter for the municipality. Agricultural entrepreneurs determine themselves how best to organize their business. The current Transition Fund for Future-Oriented Agriculture provides support in making the step to a sustainable future.

230596.explanation: Repairing broken appliances, clothing, or bicycles is always preferred over throwing away and buying new ones. Therefore, a repair café is a great initiative that also contributes to social encounters.

230597.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to build according to need, providing suitable housing for every resident of the municipality of Staphorst. If it turns out that there is more demand for homes for single-person households, we should build more of those.

230600.explanation: There is regularly excessive speeding in our municipality, such as on Oude Rijksweg/Gemeenteweg, in residential neighborhoods, and in rural areas. More speed controls increase the chance of being caught and help reduce the number of speeding violations.

230601.explanation: The ChristenUnie embraces initiatives for a farewell house in Staphorst. Bereaved families deserve the space to say goodbye to their loved ones in a way that suits them.

230602.explanation: Every person has value and every person wants to be valuable! The ChristenUnie supports social employment companies such as Pro Werktalent, Noggus & Noggus, and Reestmond.

230603.explanation: The center of Staphorst is already very busy, and we want to prevent it from becoming even busier. We think it is important to make the center more attractive, but not at the expense of parking spaces. We urgently need all the parking spots there!

230604.explanation: At this moment, we see no added value in a sports company, because our sports clubs are perfectly capable of proudly and passionately managing and maintaining their sports facilities with the help of many volunteers.

230605.explanation: We endorse the GGD’s call to vaccinate children. However, it always remains the parents' own choice whether or not to vaccinate their child. We believe it is important that good and objective information is available so parents can make a well-considered decision.

230606.explanation: We do not want houses, especially monuments, to deteriorate due to vacancy. A vacancy levy prevents deterioration of houses and helps reduce the housing shortage. A good idea according to the ChristenUnie.

230608.explanation: Many events are organized by dedicated volunteers and sponsors from our community. They then face significant costs for permits. We believe we should appreciate their effort by not charging permit fees for events.

230548.explanation: Raising the property tax (OZB) is not a goal in itself but is seen as a last resort.

230549.explanation: The economy is the engine of society. We are proud of our entrepreneurs and there must be enough space for them. We are thinking of new business parks, but also revitalization of existing business parks.

230550.explanation: We stand for fewer rules for entrepreneurs.

230551.explanation: We value one day a week for rest and relaxation and do not see a need for extended opening hours.

230552.explanation: We already have a lot of natural areas and do not see the need for expansion.

230553.explanation: It is important that newcomers integrate into our society by learning the language. This is the responsibility of the newcomer, but the municipality must also contribute. In addition, learning a language in practice, through having a job, is important.

230554.explanation: We acknowledge the legal obligations from the Distribution Act (Spreidingswet), but believe shelter is only responsible if it fits the scale, livability, and support within the community.

230555.explanation: We support less regulatory pressure. As a municipality, we want to commit to maintaining the number of trees. Private individuals are free to decide on this themselves.

230557.explanation: We find it important that our municipality has a good swimming pool and investments are needed for this.

230558.explanation: We see the ABC swimming diploma primarily as a parental responsibility. Besides gym classes, we regard swimming as a good addition, for example in the form of a refresher course.

230560.explanation: We value culture and heritage education. Those who do not know their roots find it hard to grow. That is why we want children to learn about the regional language and traditions of Staphorst.

230562.explanation: We find it important that there is a social and/or economic connection to our municipality.

230563.explanation: The law requires that 30% of newly built homes be social rental housing. We find it important that building meets actual needs.

230565.explanation: There must remain space for private rental housing, as this also meets a need. For starter homes, we find an owner-occupancy requirement sensible.

230566.explanation: We advocate for fewer rules. We want to preserve the character of the region.

230567.explanation: We want to preserve holiday parks for recreation and tourism.

230568.explanation: We find a coffeeshop does not fit the character of the Staphorst community.

230570.explanation: We are already making large investments and find it important to do what is necessary. Investing itself is not a goal.

230571.explanation: We believe there are sufficient charging points realized. If there is a shared need within a neighborhood, we do want to facilitate that.

230572.explanation: This contributes to a livable center with a good business climate.

230573.explanation: We want more, but smaller stops where smaller buses can also run on demand. This makes travel more flexible for passengers.

230575.explanation: We find it important that each village has a meeting place, but it does not have to be in this form, and we prefer a living-room function.

230576.explanation: It is a good tool that must be available within everything a care professional needs.

230577.explanation: More foster parents in our own municipality means children can remain in their familiar environment.

230578.explanation: We find it important that children learn about the dangers of social media.

230579.explanation: We believe confused persons should not be housed together, but that customized care must be applied.

230581.explanation: We currently have sufficient facilities for that.

230582.explanation: We see advantages in concentrating sports. The demand must come from the grassroots, and we will not impose this as a municipality.

230583.explanation: We find it important that every village has an activity field.

230584.explanation: We believe this does not belong to the municipality’s tasks, but more to the Chamber of Commerce.

230585.explanation: We appreciate the effort, but not everything can be measured in money.

230586.explanation: We do not see this as a municipal task.

230587.explanation: We have a youth council we are proud of and see no added value in a child mayor.

230588.explanation: Although we appreciate the project, it requires extra effort from the municipal staff. We already struggle to fill vacancies and must handle additional tasks carefully.

230589.explanation: This is a beautiful central location where seniors, for example, can live well close to amenities. The municipal depot also fits better in an industrial area.

230590.explanation: It is important that the fire station is located in an easily accessible place, and that does not have to be at the municipal depot location. This location is also very suitable for housing development.

230592.explanation: We do not see the necessity.

230593.explanation: There are already financial arrangements from the national government; we do not find it necessary for the municipality to contribute additional funds.

230594.explanation: As a rural municipality, a car is indispensable, and therefore you must be able to park it.

230595.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

230596.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

230597.explanation: There is a need for that, and we want to provide for it.

230600.explanation: We want to improve traffic safety, for example on school routes.

230601.explanation: We believe it is important that everyone can say goodbye to a loved one in their own way. There must be space for both lying in state and holding a farewell service. Therefore, we advocate for a farewell house near the cemetery in Staphorst.

230602.explanation: As a municipality, we want to support social entrepreneurs and think along with them. Financially supporting a specific group of entrepreneurs is not a task for the municipality.

230603.explanation: We find it important that terraces should be possible for livability; this does not have to come at the expense of all parking spaces.

230604.explanation: We see added value in a sports company responsible for managing municipal sports locations (such as the swimming pool and sports halls). Additionally, sports clubs can join at the administrative level to reduce the administrative burden on volunteers.

230605.explanation: A high vaccination rate is of great importance for public health. Vaccination protects not only the individual but also society as a whole. Therefore, we focus on reliable information. At the same time, we respect each individual's freedom of choice.

230606.explanation: In this time of housing shortage, we see this as a tool we can deploy. Our concern is that owners come up with feasible plans and can demonstrate that they are working on them.

230608.explanation: We are proud of all the events that are organized, because celebrating, experiencing, and connecting together is indispensable to us!

230548.explanation: Municipal Interests opposes an increase in the property tax (OZB). First, critical scrutiny of potential savings within the municipal organization must take place. Residents should not automatically bear higher charges as long as savings are possible elsewhere.

230549.explanation: A limited expansion of industrial sites in Staphorst can be considered, provided it aligns with local activities and is carefully coordinated with the housing supply, livability, and the preservation of the municipality’s rural character.

230550.explanation: Cafes and restaurants can, in principle, determine their own opening hours based on their responsibility as entrepreneurs. This should include respect for Sunday rest and the living environment so that entrepreneurship and social values remain carefully balanced.

230551.explanation: The fact that the shops in the village of Staphorst are closed aligns with local values and traditions, but there must be room for careful consideration in any future developments.

230552.explanation: It is undesirable to actively encourage the conversion of farmland into nature reserves, as this can hinder agricultural businesses in their operations. A vital and future-proof agricultural business requires sufficient space to operate and develop.

230553.explanation: It is not necessary for the municipality to allocate additional resources here, as newcomers already have their own budget for this. It is more effective to actively encourage and guide participation in language lessons since this has a greater impact on actual language development than additional spending.

230554.explanation: Although the obligations under the dispersion law are acknowledged, it is not desirable to establish a reception center (azc) in our municipality. The community’s capacity and the impact on livability require caution and solutions that do justice to the local situation as much as possible.

230555.explanation: A general obligation is not necessary because the statutory replanting duty already regulates this within the municipality. Tailoring per situation offers more room to implement replanting carefully and appropriately within local circumstances.

230557.explanation: Municipal Interests wants to maintain a good swimming pool for our municipality. Research shows that new construction at this location is preferable to adapting the existing pool, both in terms of sustainability and future-proofing.

230558.explanation: Responsibility for school swimming lies with the schools. It is important that children receive swimming lessons as this is vital for their safety and development. Municipal Interests supports the reinstatement of school swimming.

230560.explanation: There is reason to doubt the mandatory promotion of dialect education at all primary schools. Although preserving culture and tradition is important, this must be balanced against the regular curriculum and the schools’ freedom to shape their educational offerings appropriately.

230562.explanation: It is positive that local housing seekers can be given priority, but the final allocation lies with the housing corporation De Vechthorst. Careful consideration remains necessary to fairly serve both local housing seekers and other stakeholders.

230563.explanation: The municipality of Staphorst distributes rental and owner-occupied homes according to the Multi-Year Housing Program 2030: approximately 30% social rental, 40% affordable owner-occupied, and 30% higher-priced owner-occupied, aimed at demand, retention of starters, and balance in the housing market. Municipal Interests supports this policy.

230565.explanation: The municipality of Staphorst already has an owner-occupancy obligation of at least 5 years for buyers of building plots. Municipal Interests believes this should apply to all homes to counter speculation and stabilize the housing market.

230566.explanation: It is undesirable to abolish the aesthetic regulations. These rules protect the character, quality, and living environment of the municipality, ensure coherence in construction and renovation, and prevent undesirable and harmful developments in both village and landscape views.

230567.explanation: The municipality does not allow the use of holiday homes as a permanent residence. Exceptions are possible for existing, long-term occupancy through a personal exemption. Additionally, a holiday home may only be used as an emergency solution for a maximum period of two years.

230568.explanation: Given current laws and regulations and Municipal Interests’ position that an entrepreneur is free to choose their activity, the establishment of a coffee shop may be permitted within legal conditions, but Municipal Interests is not in favor of this.

230570.explanation: Municipal Interests believes that sufficient financial resources must be made available for well-founded and effective youth work. Investing in youth work contributes to prevention, development, and social engagement of young people.

230571.explanation: The municipality must provide more charging points for electric cars to promote sustainable mobility. It is important that the installation of these does not come at the expense of existing parking spaces and that accessibility and parking capacity are maintained.

230572.explanation: Parking in the center of Staphorst must remain free. Free parking removes barriers for visitors and attracts shoppers, which increases accessibility and supports local businesses.

230573.explanation: Municipal Interests Staphorst supports good public transport. Therefore, it should be investigated whether demand-responsive transport between villages, such as a dial-a-ride bus, is desirable and feasible to improve accessibility and mobility for all residents.

230575.explanation: It is desirable that all villages have a social and cultural community center. Smart combinations of multiple functions should be sought so that facilities are efficiently used and contribute to meeting, cohesion, and livability within the villages.

230576.explanation: Free couples therapy is not a core municipal task but can be used if necessary as a preventive measure to support relationships and prevent larger social problems. Municipal Interests sees a supporting role for the municipality for existing organizations in this regard.

230577.explanation: The municipality must invest more in recruiting foster parents. The nationally determined contribution can be supplemented by the municipality when demand exceeds supply to ensure sufficient foster care and provide children with a stable and safe environment.

230578.explanation: Sexually explicit messaging education is primarily a task of the national government. However, the municipality of Staphorst can support this policy by facilitating local campaigns or educational programs. If necessary, Municipal Interests will vote for financial support.

230579.explanation: Gemeentebelangen is positive about shared living spaces for mentally disturbed individuals. Since funding is provided through the Social Support Act (Wmo) and the Long-term Care Act (Wlz), and implementation is handled by specialized organizations, the municipality can play a facilitating and supportive role to ensure appropriate care and guidance.

230581.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants the municipality to invest in appropriate support, as research shows increasing pressure on informal caregivers leading to overload. Extra attention and facilities help informal caregivers to persevere and contribute to sustainable care within society.

230582.explanation: The municipality should not relocate the riding school to the event site De Tippe. Although consolidation of sports facilities is supported, riding schools are not included. Retaining it at the current location is more appropriate given the nature of the facility and the function of De Tippe.

230583.explanation: It is desirable to establish an event site in IJhorst. By combining this with a multifunctional accommodation (MFA), facilities can be shared efficiently, contributing to livability, social cohesion, and wider usage by clubs and residents.

230584.explanation: Gemeentebelangen supports attention to a starters' desk. The municipality already has an entrepreneurs' desk, but it is important to maintain it well and keep it up to date so that starting entrepreneurs receive accessible and effective support.

230585.explanation: It is important to show appreciation to schools and associations that clean up litter, but structural financial compensation is not desirable. Cleaning up litter should not become a business model; recognition and support can also take place in other ways.

230586.explanation: It is desirable that the municipality supports bicycle lessons for refugees with residence status. By facilitating initiatives from local partners, self-reliance is increased, integration is promoted, and safe participation in traffic is enabled.

230587.explanation: Appointing a children's mayor can contribute to the involvement of children and youth, but it is not a necessary measure. The municipality of Staphorst can organize this input in other ways, for example through schools or existing participation forms.

230588.explanation: Gemeentebelangen finds it desirable that the municipality of Staphorst participates in Politiekids. This project increases children's understanding of and appreciation for police work, promotes respect for authority, and fosters mutual trust between youth and police.

230589.explanation: It is definitely desirable that space is made available for new homes at the municipal yard location in Staphorst. This contributes to solving the housing demand particularly among seniors, strengthens livability, and efficiently utilizes available space within the core of Staphorst.

230590.explanation: Merging with the municipal yard and possibly other municipal services is strongly preferred by Gemeentebelangen because it promotes efficiency, cooperation, and multifunctional use of space without reducing operational safety.

230592.explanation: Gemeentebelangen is neutral towards creating more fishing spots. If fishing associations submit a concrete request, Gemeentebelangen wants to support it, provided it fits within available space, regulations, and preserves natural values in the municipality of Staphorst.

230593.explanation: It is good that the municipality supports companies that employ people with disabilities. Within the frameworks of the Participation Act, financial incentives can contribute to inclusion, labor participation, and equal opportunities in the labor market, so more residents find valuable jobs.

230594.explanation: Gemeentebelangen opposes this. The number of cars per inhabitant is higher than the national average, and public transport is insufficiently developed, which would lead to extra parking pressure in the municipality and endanger the accessibility of homes.

230595.explanation: The municipality of Staphorst has the Transition Fund for Future-Oriented Agriculture 2025–2028. This scheme is supported by Gemeentebelangen.

230596.explanation: The municipality does not need to establish a repair café; this is not a core task. A repair café is a good extension of existing thrift shops and can be set up by volunteers and social organizations without requiring municipal funds.

230597.explanation: It is good that the municipality, together with housing corporations, realizes more homes suitable for single persons. This way, the housing stock better matches current demand, enabling singles to find appropriate housing and promoting livability and flow within the municipality.

230600.explanation: It is desirable that the municipality asks the police to carry out more speed checks. This contributes to traffic safety, prevents nuisance for residents, and strengthens the feeling of safety on roads within the municipality.

230601.explanation: Realizing a farewell house is not a core task of the municipality but a commercial activity. The municipality can facilitate by providing space or procedures, so entrepreneurs have the opportunity to develop a suitable farewell house near the cemetery.

230602.explanation: Financial support of social entrepreneurs is desirable. Within the frameworks of the Participation Act, this contributes to employment for people distant from the labor market, promotes social participation, and strengthens the social and economic structure within the municipality.

230603.explanation: Gemeentebelangen supports the free parking policy in our municipality. If an entrepreneur has a plan to operate a terrace on a parking space, this must be compensated with a parking space at another location.

230604.explanation: Taking over sports facilities by a professional sports company is not desirable. Further commercialization does not fit the club culture. Sports clubs are well capable of deciding on cooperation, merger, or takeover themselves without mandatory external control.

230605.explanation: It is desirable that the municipality encourages residents to vaccinate their children. Respecting residents' own choices, the benefits such as protection of public health and vulnerable groups can be clearly emphasized.

230606.explanation: Gemeentebelangen opposes prolonged vacancy of homes. Even though Staphorst does not apply vacancy laws or local regulations, the principle remains that homes must be available for habitation to counteract housing shortages.

230608.explanation: Abolishing permit fees for events is not desirable. The principle 'the user pays' remains in effect. Fees may not exceed the costs incurred but also should not be fully borne by the community.

230548.explanation: This is to maintain facilities at standard, ensuring those who benefit most carry the heaviest burdens.

230549.explanation: There is still plenty in the pipeline.

230551.explanation: In the core of Staphorst this is fine; only in the surrounding villages this should be left free.

230552.explanation: The existing nature must remain intact. Agricultural land can be converted to nature as compensation for the expansion of the ammunition depot.

230554.explanation: Our municipality must also comply with the national spreading law.

230557.explanation: The swimming pool in our municipality of Staphorst must remain and we are always in favor of sustainability.

230558.explanation: All children must be able to swim.

230560.explanation: Not a core task of education.

230562.explanation: There is no fence around Staphorst.

230563.explanation: Everyone must be able to live.

230566.explanation: Otherwise it will become a mess.

230567.explanation: The purpose of a house must be clear.

230568.explanation: Cannabis is less addictive than alcohol.

230570.explanation: It is worth everything to invest in our children and youth.

230571.explanation: We support the greening of the vehicle fleet.

230572.explanation: Introduce a parking disc, because otherwise the parking pressure becomes too high.

230575.explanation: Can take place in multifunctional spaces. It is important for social interaction.

230578.explanation: It concerns, among other things, the DANGERS of sharing sexually explicit messages. This is the task of the welfare foundation BIND.

230579.explanation: There must be responsible care for confused persons. This can be provided both individually and in groups.

230581.explanation: We prefer mixed living arrangements, but more places must be made available for the elderly.

230582.explanation: We do not have enough information here to express an opinion.

230583.explanation: The residents of IJhorst have been solving this themselves so far.

230584.explanation: That is what the Chamber of Commerce is for.

230585.explanation: In the context of raising awareness about littering.

230586.explanation: Refugee work should receive funding to do this.

230587.explanation: There are already children's debates for the eighth grade primary school children and there is a youth council.

230588.explanation: We wholeheartedly support good projects that promote the well-being and awareness of our children. Politiekids could possibly be such a project, but there are several projects that could contribute.

230589.explanation: The municipal yard is a nice residential location in the center.

230590.explanation: Can work well on the industrial estate.

230593.explanation: This is already regulated by legislation.

230594.explanation: Additionally, a shared parking spot under a solar panel roof.

230595.explanation: We support the greening and diversification of agriculture. It is not the municipality's role to provide funds to individual farmers. However, the municipality can support joint sustainable agricultural projects.

230596.explanation: We support as much repair and reuse as possible.

230597.explanation: We support diversity in housing types.

230600.explanation: We support more safety in traffic.

230601.explanation: A farewell home does not necessarily have to be next to the cemetery.

230602.explanation: The municipality is co-owner of Reestmond and that must remain so.

230603.explanation: We support more terraces but they can be in multiple locations.

230604.explanation: The municipality is responsible for sports policy, no privatization.

230605.explanation: The vaccination rate in Staphorst is low and poses a danger to public health.

230582.explanation: As long as it is not clear what this concretely means for the location, costs, and implementation, a yes or no is premature.

230583.explanation: There must be a further examination of usefulness, necessity, and costs before this can be discussed.

statements.title: There must be an event grounds in IJhorst.
The municipality must invest in a start-up desk for questions from new entrepreneurs.
The parking lots next to the roundabout in the center of Staphorst must be converted into terraces.
The municipality must ensure that a repair café is established in Staphorst where volunteers can carry out simple repairs.
All villages in the municipality should have their own social and cultural community center.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must ensure that 30% of newly built homes are social rental housing. In the municipality of Staphorst, 10% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet ervoor zorgen dat 30% van de nieuw te bouwen woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: is. In de gemeente Staphorst is nu 10% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: The property tax (on immovable property) is one of the municipal taxes and depends on the WOZ value. The WOZ value is the value the municipality assigns to a house; WOZ stands for Valuation of Immovable Property. For homeowners in the municipality of Staphorst the rate in 2026 is 0.1005% of the WOZ value.

statements.title: The municipality should encourage residents to have their children vaccinated.
The municipality should invest more money in youth work.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality should move the riding school out of the village of Staphorst to the site of the De Tippe event grounds.
The municipality must abolish the aesthetic standards for building and renovating.

titleParts.glossary: Aesthetic regulations that state how the outside of a building may look.

statements.title: The municipality should continue to provide free couples therapy.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet blijven zorgen voor gratis

titleParts.glossary: When people try to improve their romantic relationship together with a counselor.

statements.title: The municipality should provide more charging points for electric cars.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Staphorst currently has 34 public charging stations. Each charging station has two charging points. Only electric cars may park in a spot with a charging station.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on language lessons for newcomers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan taallessen voor

titleParts.glossary: People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.theme: Housing for people with mental health issues
statements.title: The municipality should invest in shared housing for people experiencing mental health problems.
The municipality should allow the construction of family homes with one parking space.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality determines how many parking spaces are required per dwelling. In Staphorst this varies by type of home. Under current policy, family homes must have more than one parking space per dwelling.

statements.title: The site of the municipal depot in Staphorst should be made available for new housing.
statements.moreInfo: At the municipal yard on Gemeenteweg 36 are workshops and offices of the municipal outdoor service and a fire station. The municipal yard buildings are outdated and in poor condition. The municipality is conducting a council-mandated study into the future of the municipal yard.

statements.title: The municipality should financially support social entrepreneurs.

titleParts.glossary: Social entrepreneurs run a business that not only makes profit but also serves a social purpose.

statements.title: The municipality of Staphorst should participate in the Politiekids project, in which children are introduced to the work of the police.
The municipality of Staphorst should appoint a children's mayor who communicates the views of children and young people to the municipal council.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country because of war or personal persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses the application and decides whether they may stay. During the procedure asylum seekers must be accommodated. The Netherlands has a shortage of reception places; asylum seekers ideally should be housed in an asylum seekers’ center (azc), a facility set up for living with services like medical and social care and nearby schooling for children. Under the national Distribution Act the municipality of Staphorst must accommodate 101 asylum seekers, of whom 14 are unaccompanied young people.

statements.title: A farewell house for funerals should be built next to the cemetery in Staphorst.
The municipality should provide funds to farmers who want to switch from conventional to organic farming.
The fire station should remain on the site of the municipal depot in Staphorst.
The municipality should give more money to companies that employ people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld geven aan bedrijven die mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish permit fees for events.

titleParts.glossary: With a permit the municipality gives permission to do something.
A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: The municipality should provide funds to schools and associations that help clean up litter.
The municipality should encourage attention to the local dialect of Staphorst at all primary schools.
Shops in the municipality of Staphorst should remain closed on Sundays.

shootoutStatements.title: For every tree felled in the municipality, a new one must be planted within the municipality.
The municipality should invest in tailored transport between the main centre and surrounding areas (for example, a dial-a-ride bus).
The municipality must fine owners who leave a house vacant for more than a year.
More business parks must be developed in the municipality of Staphorst.

shootoutStatements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
shootoutStatements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality must make the De Broene Eugte swimming pool in Staphorst more sustainable.
The municipality must provide bicycle lessons for refugees with residence permits.
More fishing spots must be created in the municipality of Staphorst.
The municipality of Staphorst must ask the police to carry out more speed checks.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
Anyone who buys a house in the municipality of Staphorst must live in it themselves.
Parking in the center of Staphorst must remain free.
A coffeeshop may be allowed in the municipality of Staphorst.
The municipality of Staphorst must encourage converting agricultural land into nature reserves.
The municipality must invest more money in recruiting foster parents.
A sports company with professional management must take over the management and use of sports facilities from sports clubs.
The municipality must invest in education about sharing sexually explicit messages.
The municipality must invest in new living places where elderly people can be cared for together.
More homes suitable for single-person occupancy must be built in the municipality of Staphorst.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent address for older people.
